license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.sr2ed8e
title: Phycocyanin Extraction from Synechocystis
author: Sebastian ST Triesch

steps_raw.instruction: Collect 1 ml Synechocystis liquid culture and spin it down at 14,000 g for 5 min . Discard the supernatant and keep the pellets cool. 00:05:00, Centrifugation
Resuspend the pellet from step 1 in 1 ml PBS Buffer (pH = 7.4). Transfer the solution into the prepared 1.5 ml tubes filled with glass beads. Keep the solution cool.
Fill a 1.5 ml Eppendorf Tube with 400 ml glass beads (~0.5 mm) for each sample.
Thoroughly vortex each sample for 1 min . Repeat the process five times . The supernatant should become blue-green and clear. 00:01:00, Vortex
Spin down your vortexed sample for 5 min at 3,000 g at 4 °C . Transfer the supernatant into a fresh 2 ml Eppendorf tube and keep the supernatant cool. 00:05:00, Centrifugation
Spin down your supernatant again at 14,000 g for 20 min at 4 °C . 00:20:00, Centrifugation
Transfer the supernatant into a cuvette. The supernatant should appear clearly blue at this stage. Measure the extinction at 620 nm . Use PBS as a blank.
